name: My Paris delta - Marginal REVOLUTION
abstract: This article outlines a series of observations made by the author during a recent visit to Paris, comparing the current state to that in 2019. Key trends include the increased opening of shops on Sundays, the growing bilingualism (English) of Central Paris, and a surge in diverse non-European restaurants. Other points of note are the difficulty in locating gas stations, the widespread presence of tattoos among young women, and a significant decline in public smoking. The author also mentions the availability of Rainier cherries in June, the continued prevalence of high-quality bookshops, and a positive impression of the Bibliotheque National area, concluding that Paris is thriving.
